Argentina.form_note: Won 3-0 vs Algeria (Messi hat-trick, equalled WC all-time goals record). Won 2-0 vs Austria (Messi 38', 90+5') — Messi now holds the WC all-time scoring record with 18 goals. Argentina top Group J and through to R32. Next vs Jordan June 27.

injuries.note: Fractured right ring finger May 20 (Europa League final vs Freiburg); missed warm-ups vs Honduras and Iceland; returned to full glove training June 12 and confirmed to start vs Algeria
Soleus muscle injury suffered June 6 in training camp; Marseille captain required late replacement
Required surgery, ruled out of the tournament
Knee injury
Knee injury
Fully recovered from MCL sprain
Overcame a relatively minor hamstring injury; expected to lead the team in the opener
Minor quadriceps strain; ruled out of the Austria match (June 22) — Nahuel Molina starts at right back; targeting return for Jordan game

key_players.note: 12 goals and 7 assists in the 2026 MLS season (avg FotMob rating 8.47); turns 39 during tournament, his sixth and final World Cup
First-choice striker; part of an Atletico-heavy attacking contingent (with Almada, N. Gonzalez, G. Simeone)
Named in final squad as a senior forward option
Creative option; one of 17 returning players from the 2022 title-winning squad

news: Squad features 17 of the 26 players who won the 2022 title in Qatar; Argentina aim to become first back-to-back champions since Brazil in 1962.
Considered to have drawn a kind group (Algeria, Austria, Jordan); analysts argue they are underpriced as only sixth favorite on odds boards.
Messi hat-trick vs Algeria (3-0) in the Group J opener; his first WC hat-trick and ties the all-time WC goals record.
Messi scored twice vs Austria (2-0) to set new all-time WC scoring record (18 goals total). Montiel missed Austria match with quad strain; targeting return vs Jordan June 27.

France.form_note: Won 3-1 vs Senegal (Mbappe 2, Barcola). Won 3-0 vs Iraq (Mbappe 2, Dembele) through the tournament's first rain delay — Mbappe now has 16 WC career goals, tied 2nd all-time. France top Group I and through to R32. Next vs Norway June 26.

injuries.note: Achilles tendon rupture in April (slipped during Liverpool's UCL exit vs PSG); recovery extends into late 2026
Knee injury, ruled out for the season

Overcame a muscular scare; ready at right-back for the Senegal opener

key_players.note: 48 goals and 10 assists in 2025-26 (29 in 24 La Liga matches, 15 in 11 UCL apps); chasing France and World Cup all-time scoring records
Reigning Ballon d'Or holder (35g/14a in 2024-25); just won a second Champions League title vs Arsenal on May 30
Competing for a starting berth in a stacked attack with Doue, Cherki and Thuram
Part of PSG's Champions League-winning core arriving in peak form

news: This tournament is Didier Deschamps' swansong as manager after two consecutive finals; squad motivated to send him off with a third star.
Arguably the deepest roster in the 48-team field, especially in attack.
Group I with Senegal, Norway and Iraq — the standout 'group of death' candidate among top seeds.

Spain.form_note: Drew 0-0 vs Cape Verde (June 15). Won 4-0 vs Saudi Arabia (June 21). Top of Group H, through to R32. Next vs Uruguay June 26.

news: Squad contains no Real Madrid players for the first time in World Cup history.

England.form_note: Won 4-2 vs Croatia in WC opener at AT&T Stadium (Kane 2, Bellingham, Rashford; Kramaric, Modric for Croatia). Top of Group L. Today vs Ghana June 23.

injuries.note: Hamstring injury from late-April Merseyside derby

news: Thomas Tuchel's ruthless squad cuts: Cole Palmer, Phil Foden, Trent Alexander-Arnold, Harry Maguire and Adam Wharton all left out — biggest selection story of the build-up.
Ivan Toney and Ollie Watkins named as the back-up strikers to Kane.
ESPN power-rankings panel gave England zero first-place votes amid uncertainty over Tuchel's selections despite the flawless qualifying record.

Brazil.form_note: Drew 1-1 with Morocco. Won 3-0 vs Haiti (Raphinha limped off 38th min with thigh injury). Tomorrow vs Scotland June 24. Raphinha confirmed OUT; Neymar set to RETURN for Scotland match.

Germany.form_note: Won 7-1 vs Curaçao. Won 2-1 vs Ivory Coast (June 20, Undav injury-time winner). Top of Group E, through to R32. Next vs Ecuador June 25.

Belgium.form_note: Drew 1-1 vs Egypt (June 15). Drew 0-0 vs Iran (June 21). 2 pts — must beat New Zealand June 26 to advance.

Aymen Hussein was held for questioning at Chicago airport for 7 hours before being cleared to enter the US ahead of the Norway opener.

Algeria.form_note: Lost 0-3 to Argentina. Won 2-1 vs Jordan (trailed 0-1; Benbouali 68', Gouiri 82' from Mahrez assist to complete comeback). Algeria alive in Group J, need result vs Argentina June 27.

injuries.note: Recovered from a facial injury; first-choice goalkeeper for the opener vs Argentina

key_players.note: Captain and chief creator
Zinedine Zidane's son, choosing Algeria, starts in goal

news: AFCON 2025 winners momentum; the Argentina opener is the marquee tie of Group J.

Austria.form_note: Won 3-1 vs Jordan. Lost 0-2 to Argentina (both Messi goals set new WC scoring record). Austria still 2nd in Group J and through to R32. Next vs Jordan in final group game June 27.

injuries.note: Right thigh injury in pre-tournament warm-up vs Tunisia; required surgery — ruled out of entire tournament, replaced in squad by Ljubicic

Broken jaw suffered vs Jordan opener (collision); initially feared tournament over, but fitted with protective brace — Rangnick confirmed June 21 'He is ready to play tomorrow just like any other player'; available for Argentina match

key_players.note: Captain at his first World Cup
All-time record scorer; broke the record during qualification
20-year-old winger; 3 goals in 22 appearances in a productive first PSV season

news: Ralf Rangnick's pressing side is a popular pick to join Argentina out of Group J.
2026-06-22 pre-match: Posch cleared to play despite broken jaw (protective brace fitted; went through full training). Baumgartner (surgery) out for tournament; Ljubicic called up as replacement.

Jordan.form_note: Lost 1-3 to Austria. Lost 1-2 to Algeria (led 1-0 through Al-Rashdan, but Algeria equalized and won 82'). Jordan eliminated from Group J. Last game vs Argentina June 27.

injuries.note: Injured since December; top scorer in qualifying with 8 goals — ruled out of the entire tournament
Injury; ruled out of the tournament

key_players.note: Star winger from the 2023 Asian Cup final run

news: Qualified automatically by finishing second behind South Korea in their AFC group.

DR Congo.form_note: Drew 1-1 with Portugal (Wissa stoppage-time equalizer in first half; Portugal had gone ahead). Today vs Colombia June 23.
news: Won the African playoff path then the FIFA intercontinental playoff to reach a first finals in 52 years.

Uzbekistan.form_note: Lost 1-3 to Colombia in WC opener (June 17). Today vs Portugal June 23.

injuries.note: Back injury; missed Colombia opener and tournament
Missed last warm-up friendly; fitness uncertain
Came off during the Colombia defeat; slight doubt for Portugal match (June 23)

key_players.note: Centre-back and the squad's highest-profile player
Record scorer leading the line

news: Group K finale vs Colombia likely decides their knockout hopes.

Ghana.form_note: Won 1-0 vs Panama (June 17). Today vs England June 23. Thomas Partey cleared to enter USA and available.

injuries.note: Long-term injury rules out Ghana's biggest star
Long-term injury
Previously listed out due to visa complications; cleared to enter the USA — available and expected to start vs England (June 23), demotes Elisha Owusu to bench
GK taken off at halftime vs Panama with injury; expected out approximately 3 weeks; Benjamin Asare starts in goal vs England

key_players.note: Carries the main attacking burden with Kudus out

news: Losing Kudus and Salisu severely dents hopes of escaping Group L.
Thomas Partey cleared to play vs England — his visa complication resolved; significant midfield upgrade.
GK Lawrence Ati Zigi injured vs Panama, Benjamin Asare starts vs England.

Panama.form_note: Lost 0-1 to Ghana (June 17). Today vs Croatia June 23 — must win to stay alive.

injuries.note: Groin injury; MISSED the Ghana match; still doubtful for Croatia game — out for almost a month before partial return to training

key_players.note: Midfield orchestrator of the CONCACAF qualifying campaign

news: Topped their CONCACAF final-round group to qualify directly.
